evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Deploying solutions for tracking, self-service analytics, and real-time processing
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed: rigour signals like 'data quality, documentation, and versioning' and 'Single Source of Truth' sit alongside delivery-speed signals like 'real-time processing' and high-traffic environment focus, roughly at parity.
evidence.domain_risk: Revenue, ARR, MRR, margin, LTV, marketing performance
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Business/commercial metrics affecting decisions without explicit regulatory, audit, or financial-reporting compliance language, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Owning the complete data lifecycle: ingestion, transformation, visualization, and reverse ETL
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The candidate owns the entire data lifecycle and constructs the warehouse from scratch as 'Single Source of Truth', indicating the data function is still being established.
evidence.jd_authorship: Managing tracking plan definition and feature coverage
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Airbyte, Rudderstack, Dagster, Quicksight, Mixpanel, Redshift) and specific metrics (ARR, MRR, LTV, cohort analysis) make the day-to-day work clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Revenue, ARR, MRR, margin, LTV, marketing performance
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Metrics focus centers on revenue, marketing performance, and commercial growth indicators, placing this in commercial orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Owning the complete data lifecycle: ingestion, transformation, visualization, and reverse ETL
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role owns the full data stack build-out and defines the tracking plan and Single Source of Truth, which is direction-setting rather than scoped execution.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Enhancing data quality, documentation, and versioning
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality is mentioned as an area to enhance but without explicit ownership verbs framing a formal testing practice, and no dedicated testing tool named, so tool_listed as a loose signal.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: None: the JD is framed entirely in delivery and capability-building terms with no risk register or compliance language.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role builds the warehouse and data lifecycle from scratch as a 'Single Source of Truth', a clear greenfield signal.
